metadata.title: Empirical Geometric Relationships Between Gauge Couplings and Cosmological Parameters
metadata.description: We present empirical relationships connecting Standard Model gauge couplings and cosmological density parameters through geometric factors arising from the Friedmann equations. Starting from a phenomenological entropy functional, we find Omega_Lambda/Omega_m = sqrt(3*pi/2) = 2.171 (0.04% precision). This connects to electroweak physics through theta_W = pi/6, yielding sin^2(theta_W) = 1/4 at tree level. The strong coupling follows as alpha_s = Omega_Lambda/Z where Z = 2*sqrt(8*pi/3) = 5.789 (0.3% precision). Most remarkably, we empirically discover alpha_em = 1/(4*Z^2 + 3) = 1/137.04 (0.004% precision). The observed weak mixing angle sin^2(theta_W) = 1/4 - alpha_s/(2*pi) = 0.2312 matches experiment to 0.01%. We also find Omega_b = alpha_em*(Z+1) (0.5% precision). Fermion masses follow m_f = m_W * sqrt(3*pi/2)^n with integer n, explaining the mass hierarchy. Statistical caveats, look-elsewhere effects, and falsifiable predictions are discussed. Version 4 includes all three gauge couplings, baryon density, fermion mass structure, and honest assessment of theoretical gaps.
references: Particle Data Group (2024). Phys. Rev. D 110, 030001
Hosotani et al. (2024). Sp(6) Gauge-Higgs Unification. arXiv:2411.02808
Cheng et al. (2002). SU(3) Electroweak Unification. arXiv:hep-ph/0202107
Gatto, Sartori, Tonin (1968). Phys. Lett. B 28, 128
Velten et al. (2014). Eur. Phys. J. C 74, 3160
metadata.notes: Version 4: Complete framework with all three gauge couplings derived, baryon density formula, fermion mass power law structure. Includes rigorous self-review addressing statistical caveats, look-elsewhere effects, relationship dependencies, and honest acknowledgment of theoretical gaps requiring future work.
